text_original: That which is at rest is easily kept hold of;
text_normalized: That which is at rest is easily kept hold of;

text_original: before a thing has given indications of its presence, it is easy to take measures against it;
text_normalized: before a thing has given indications of its presence, it is easy to take measures against it;

text_original: that which is brittle is easily broken;
text_normalized: that which is brittle is easily broken;

text_original: that which is very small is easily dispersed.
text_normalized: that which is very small is easily dispersed.

text_original: Action should be taken before a thing has made its appearance;
text_normalized: Action should be taken before a thing has made its appearance;

text_original: order should be secured before disorder has begun.
text_normalized: order should be secured before disorder has begun.

text_original: The tree which fills the arms grew from the tiniest sprout;
text_normalized: The tree which fills the arms grew from the tiniest sprout;

text_original: the tower of nine storeys rose from a (small) heap of earth;
text_normalized: the tower of nine storeys rose from a (small) heap of earth;

text_original: the journey of a thousand li commenced with a single step.
text_normalized: the journey of a thousand li commenced with a single step.

text_original: He who acts (with an ulterior purpose) does harm;
text_normalized: He who acts (with an ulterior purpose) does harm;

text_original: he who takes hold of a thing (in the same way) loses his hold.
text_normalized: he who takes hold of a thing (in the same way) loses his hold.

text_original: The sage does not act (so), and therefore does no harm;
text_normalized: The sage does not act (so), and therefore does no harm;

text_original: he does not lay hold (so), and therefore does not lose his bold.
text_normalized: he does not lay hold (so), and therefore does not lose his bold.

text_original: (But) people in their conduct of affairs are constantly ruining them when they are on the eve of success.
text_normalized: (But) people in their conduct of affairs are constantly ruining them when they are on the eve of success.

text_original: If they were careful at the end, as (they should be) at the beginning, they would not so ruin them.
text_normalized: If they were careful at the end, as (they should be) at the beginning, they would not so ruin them.

text_original: Therefore the sage desires what (other men) do not desire, and does not prize things difficult to get;
text_normalized: Therefore the sage desires what (other men) do not desire, and does not prize things difficult to get;

text_original: he learns what (other men) do not learn, and turns back to what the multitude of men have passed by.
text_normalized: he learns what (other men) do not learn, and turns back to what the multitude of men have passed by.

text_original: Thus he helps the natural development of all things, and does not dare to act (with an ulterior purpose of his own).
text_normalized: Thus he helps the natural development of all things, and does not dare to act (with an ulterior purpose of his own).
